hook.text: In Baltimore, life expectancy drops 20 years between two neighborhoods six miles apart. Poverty is a health crisis.

buildup.text: Poor communities have fewer hospitals, worse air quality, limited healthy food, and higher exposure to violence.

discovery.text: Chronic stress from poverty triggers cortisol overload, raising the risk of heart disease, diabetes, and depression.

twist.text: Universal healthcare helps, but it can't fix toxic environments, food deserts, and the daily grind of financial strain.

climax.text: Health inequality is stratification made physical. Your body keeps score of the social position you occupy.

punchline.text: Poverty doesn't just empty wallets. It shortens lives.

quiz.question: How does poverty affect health?
options: Chronic stress and worse environments raise disease risk
Poverty has no measurable effect on health
Only mental health is affected by poverty
